subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear introductionâmethodâbodyâconclusion structure enables logical flow
Consistent use of headings, subâheadings and summary tables improves organization
Mathematical thread is easy to trace thanks to numbered equations and labelled figures
weaknesses: Occasional jumps in reasoning (e.g. skipped algebraic steps, abrupt topic shifts) interrupt flow
Repetitive explanatory prose and non-essential background material reduce conciseness
Some pageâreference misalignments and awkwardly split tables detract from polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the work solidly in band 3 for coherence and organization, with mostly relevant content but occasional digressions. Minor interruptions prevent an excellent score, so a 3/4 reflects well-developed structure with room for greater conciseness.

strengths: Mostly consistent and correct use of mathematical notation (Ï, subscripts, calculus symbols)
Effective combination of algebraic derivations, graphs, diagrams and tables
Well-ordered argument steps (definition â derivation â test â interpretation) ensure understandable flow
weaknesses: Inconsistent variable naming (e.g. uppercase vs lowercase, reused symbols)
Omitted intermediate algebraic steps reduce clarity for some readers
Lengthy derivations and missing unit annotations hamper precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands are rated band 3: mathematical language is used consistently though not flawlessly, representations are effective, and logical communication is clear. The exploration demonstrates good mathematical communication overall, meriting 3/4.

strengths: Clear link to personal interests in business and environment
Use of real-world products (Coke can example) shows initiative
Application of Hessian tests and sensitivity analysis goes beyond basic tasks
weaknesses: Mathematical techniques follow standard textbook approaches rather than novel adaptations
Creative choices are solid but not outstandingly personal or innovative
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strands show significant independent thinking, a clear personal approach and good creativity (band 2 across the board). The work surpasses minimal engagement but does not reach the highest level of personal innovation, so a 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Dedicated sections for results summary, strengths & limitations and further research
Some reflection on sustainability implications and methodological assumptions
weaknesses: Reflection is largely descriptive rather than critically probing
Analysis of results and sensitivity study is basic with minimal depth
Evaluation of outcomes lacks quantitative benchmarking or concrete future implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Depth of reflection meets band 2, but critical analysis and evaluation of outcomes remain at band 1. With two strands limited and only one reaching meaningful reflection, the overall Reflection score is 1/3.

strengths: Relevant use of calculus optimisations, second-derivative tests and Lagrange multipliers
Correct application of Hessian matrix confirms expertise in multivariable methods
Clear derivation of optimal relations (e.g. h_opt = 2 r_opt) demonstrates solid understanding
weaknesses: Minor algebraic slips and typographical inconsistencies in notation
Occasional mis-calculations of units and omitted rule annotations reduce accuracy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the mathematics is wholly relevant and largely at course level, minor errors and inconsistencies keep it at âgood understanding with minor errorsâ (band 3). The level appropriateness reaches band 4, but overall the criterion sits at 3/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are generally ordered: definition â derivation â test for minimum â real-life example â interpretation.  Reasoning is understandable, although some algebra is dense and a few explanatory sentences are awkwardly phrased.  Overall clarity is good, supporting a âlogical structure and clear communicationâ rating.

Derivations for cylinders and cones are correct; the Lagrange solution is essentially correct though minor algebraic slips appear in the sensitivity section.  Explanations show good conceptual understanding, but the occasional mis-calculation of units and typographical errors reduce accuracy, fitting âgood understanding with minor errorsâ.

The student employs algebraic equations, Desmos graphs, hand-drawn diagrams, and summary tables.  Each representation is connected to the others (e.g. graph confirms algebraic minimum, table summarises percent waste), demonstrating effective multi-representation even if graphical quality is modest.

The exploration follows a clear introduction-method-body-conclusion sequence, and the mathematics inside each section builds logically from simple (cylinder) to more complex (prism with Lagrange multipliers).  Headings, sub-headings and summary tables help the reader to follow the argument, so the overall thread is easy to trace.  Minor jumps (e.g. skipping algebraic steps or suddenly switching to sensitivity analysis) occasionally interrupt the flow, preventing the piece from feeling âexcellentâ, but coherence is consistently good rather than merely adequate.

Using Lagrange multipliers and a Hessian check for prisms plus a sensitivity analysis indicates creativity and initiative beyond the minimum requirement.  However, these techniques are standard HL syllabus extensions rather than novel adaptations, placing the work solidly in the âgoodâ band.

Analysis of results is basic: the student calculates wasted area and comments that it is âsignificantâ, but there is little probing of why differences occur beyond surface remarks.  Sensitivity analysis results are listed, yet interpretation is brief.  This aligns with âbasic analysisâ only.

While the student lists limitations and suggests studying irregular shapes, there is no quantitative evaluation of error size or practical feasibility.  Future implications are stated in general terms.  Hence the evaluation is âlimitedâ rather than clear or comprehensive.

The student reflects on findings in the Results Summary, Strengths & Limitations and Further Research sections, commenting on sustainability implications and on methodological assumptions.  Reflection is present throughout, but tends to be descriptive rather than deeply critical, meeting the âmeaningfulâ level.

Equation numbering, correct use of Ï, subscripts (r_opt, A_wasted) and calculus notation are present throughout.  Minor inconsistencies such as switching between uppercase and lowercase v/V or omitting differential symbols in a few places prevent it from being flawless, but appropriate notation is used âmostly consistentlyâ.

The exploration is clearly the studentâs own: they link the topic to personal studies in Business and environmental interests, select their own real products, and decide the order of shapes.  This demonstrates a âclear personal approachâ, though it is not especially innovative.

Most material is directly related to minimising packaging; however, long algebraic derivations are shown in full even when standard, and explanatory prose is sometimes repetitive (e.g. restating the meaning of sensitivity analysis several times).  Occasional digressions about sustainability statistics add background but do not advance the mathematics.  Hence the piece is âmostly relevantâ but not fully concise.

Optimisation via calculus, second-derivative tests, Lagrange multipliers and Hessian matrices are all directly relevant to minimising surface areaâno extraneous mathematics is included.  The mathematics is appropriate and purposefully applied, fitting the descriptor ârelevant mathematics used appropriatelyâ.

Choosing everyday packages and extending textbook optimisation with a Hessian test shows genuine independent thought, surpassing a minimal approach.  Nevertheless, the mathematics largely mirrors standard calculus exercises and the sensitivity study follows a routine template, so the independence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

A contents page, numbered equations and labelled figures give the work a recognisable structure.  Each major task is placed in its own subsection, and the student usually signposts the purpose of that subsection at the start.  Page references occasionally mis-align and some tables split awkwardly, but the exploration is clearly laid out with identifiable introduction, body and conclusion, satisfying the descriptor for âwell organisedâ.

The work reaches into HL territory with multivariable optimisation and sensitivity analysis.  These methods are solidly within and occasionally beyond HL curriculum expectations, so the mathematics is âfully commensurate with course levelâ.

annotations.comment: Definitions table is thorough, but listing units (cm) with each symbol would improve precision.
Numerical substitution is correct, but rounding rationale should be explained to justify two decimal places.
Explanation of Desmos graph is detailed; however, graph quality appears low resolution, consider improving image clarity.
Formula for A_wasted% is introduced, but label âA_optimisedâ inconsistent with earlier âA_optâ; maintain consistent notation.
Good attempt to evaluate impact, but could include error margins or contextual benchmarks to deepen outcome analysis.
Heading clearly demarcates new mathematical section, aiding logical structure.
Skipping steps in cone derivation reduces clarity; include key intermediate steps for accessibility.
Definition of u and v in derivative is confusing: reuse consistent variable names (e.g., avoid reuse of 'v' for both volume and denominator).
Exploration title succinctly describes focus on optimisation of packaging across 3D shapes, immediately signalling mathematical content.
Subject and level clearly indicated, orienting reader to HL context.
Table of contents is comprehensive, but consider reducing entries or consolidating subsections to improve conciseness and avoid overwhelming the reader.
Heading hierarchy in TOC is clear, but inconsistent bolding of sections could be standardized for coherence.
The introduction links the topic to real-world packaging challenges and personal interests, demonstrating engagement.
Paragraph is somewhat repetitive in discussing sustainability goals; consider condensing to maintain relevance.
Methodology is detailed but lengthy; a flowchart summarizing steps could improve clarity and conciseness.
Opening symbol table lacks context; consider adding a brief introduction to guide interpretation.
Sentence ends prematurely, interrupting flow; ensure all paragraphs are complete for logical coherence.
Mathematical expressions are properly displayed using LaTeX conventions, aiding readability.
Step of cancelling 2Ï could be more explicit: show intermediate algebra to help less confident readers.
Differentiation is executed correctly, but annotation of power rule application would enhance transparency.
Derivation contains a slight typographical error in notation; ensure consistent subscripting of r_opt in formulae.
Second derivative test is applied correctly, confirming a minimum; good demonstration of calculus understanding.
Substitution for second derivative evaluation is correct, but explaining cancellation step enhances clarity.
Conclusion â12Ï > 0â is clear; consider explicitly stating âthus minimum surface areaâ to reinforce interpretation.
The relation h_opt = 2r_opt is clearly derived, demonstrating deep understanding of geometric optimization.
Practical example of Coke can engages reader and connects theory to real product, showing personal initiative.
